stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] (Δ : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) → Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α) → ∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x
priority_rationale: This is only one direction of the odd existence bridge and is weaker than broader characterization goals already visible in stronger odd-parity problems.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] (Δ : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) → (∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x) → Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This converse is a meaningful half of the odd classification picture and could feed a general criterion, but it remains only one direction.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] (Δ : Delta), Even (Delta.countReft Δ) → (∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x) → Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: The verified even-side counterexample already makes the unrestricted transfer from even fixed points to Henkin existence highly suspect, so this is not a good frontier bet.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] (n : ℕ), Odd n → ((∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act (Delta.pow [Delta.reft] n) x) ↔ Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α))
priority_rationale: This specializes to a power family and does not engage the main arbitrary-composite frontier, which is currently more structurally important.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α], IsTotal α (· ≤ ·) → ∀ (Δ : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) → ∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x
priority_rationale: This is a narrow finite-order existence statement without a sharp regime theorem or broader odd classification payoff.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] (n : ℕ), 0 < n → ((∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act (Delta.pow [Delta.box] n) x) ↔ Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α))
priority_rationale: A pure box-power equivalence is too specialized relative to the current need for corrected family-level even criteria and boundary explanations.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta) (x : α), Odd (Delta.countReft d) → x ≐ Delta.act d x → x ≐ ⊠x
priority_rationale: This pointwise normalization claim is weaker than canonical-form or uniqueness theorems and fits an overexplored local-support pattern.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta) (x : α), Even (Delta.countReft d) → x ≐ Delta.act d x → x ≐ □x
priority_rationale: This is a local even-side normalization statement that does not repair the falsified family-level bridge or isolate the source of failure.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), Even (Delta.countReft d) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∃ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h.1 ≐ Delta.act d h.1
priority_rationale: This one-way lifting from Henkin points to even composite fixed points is locally natural but does not resolve the current even-boundary bottleneck.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] (Δ : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x ↔ x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
priority_rationale: A full canonical characterization of odd fixed points for arbitrary composites would turn the odd side from scattered lemmas into a genuine classification theorem.
